name: Ambita and the Vía Verde de Tajuña — round trip
name_original: Ambita y la Vía Verde de Tajuña — ida y vuelta
region_intro: Hiking around Ambite offers a network of trails primarily following greenways and river valleys. The landscape features gentle elevation changes, often leading to viewpoints and historical sites. The region is characterized by its accessible paths, including sections of the Vía Verde de Tajuña, which provide a mix of open and semi-wooded environments. These routes are suitable for exploring the local natural and cultural heritage. Best hiking trails around Ambite The most popular hiking route is 40-Day Train Greenway Loop from Villar del Olmo, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a converted railway line, offering a smooth path. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ambita and the Vía Verde de Tajuña — round trip, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. This trail explores the Tajuña river valley, providing scenic views along the greenway. Local hikers also love the Convento Jesús del Monte Trail, a 9.4 miles (15.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the historic Convento Jesús del Monte, often completed in about 4 hours 12 minutes. Hiking around Ambite is defined by greenways, river valleys, and historical points of interest. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy hikes Ambite and moderate routes. The routes in Ambite are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Ambite's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Ambite?
A: Yes, Ambite offers several easy hiking options. The landscape features gentle elevation changes, making many paths accessible. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Balconcillo Viewpoint loop from Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.7-mile (5.9 km) route that provides a pleasant experience for those new to hiking.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes around Ambite?
A: Hikes around Ambite vary in length, with many routes falling into the moderate category. You'll find trails ranging from approximately 3.7 miles (5.9 km) to 9.4 miles (15.2 km). Durations can range from about 1 hour 40 minutes for shorter, easier paths to over 4 hours for longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025807624/convento-jesus-del-monte-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convento Jesús del Monte Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Ambite?
A: Many of Ambite's trails, particularly those along the Vía Verde de Tajuña, are suitable for families due to their relatively flat and well-maintained surfaces. These greenways offer a safe and enjoyable environment for children. Consider routes that follow converted railway lines for a smooth path.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Ambite?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Ambite, especially on the greenways and rural paths. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in areas with livestock or near roads, and to carry water for them. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the natural environment.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Ambite?
A: Yes, Ambite features several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1298714744/40-day-train-greenway-loop-from-villar-del-olmo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;40-Day Train Greenway Loop from Villar del Olmo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 6.5-mile (10.4 km) trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1298725006/ambita-and-the-via-verde-de-tajuna-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ambita and the Vía Verde de Tajuña — round trip&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views along the Tajuña river valley.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Ambite?
A: The best time for hiking in Ambite is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, while winter offers cooler temperatures, which can be suitable for hiking if you are prepared for potentially damp conditions.

Q: Is Ambite suitable for winter hiking?
A: Winter hiking in Ambite is possible, as the region generally experiences mild winters compared to more mountainous areas. The greenways and river valley paths are usually accessible. However, be prepared for cooler temperatures, and potentially muddy sections after rain. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails in Ambite?
A: Ambite's trails often lead to historical sites and natural points of interest. You might encounter landmarks such as the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Goyeneche Palace of Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2409049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pioz Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3031028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Grande de Corpa&lt;/a&gt; and various natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3255898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encina de la Pica&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cafes or refreshment stops along the hiking routes?
A: While specific cafes directly on every trail are not guaranteed, many routes pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find local establishments for refreshments. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes, and to check maps for nearby amenities before you start.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking in Ambite?
A: The river valleys and semi-wooded environments around Ambite are home to various bird species, small mammals, and insects. Keep an eye out for raptors soaring overhead, and listen for the calls of local birds. The Tajuña river itself supports aquatic life. Early mornings or late afternoons are often the best times for wildlife spotting.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Ambite?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Ambite, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's accessible paths, the scenic views along the Vía Verde de Tajuña, and the blend of natural beauty with historical points of interest. The variety of routes, from easy greenways to more challenging trails, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Ambite?
A: While Ambite is known for its gentle greenways, there are routes that offer a bit more challenge, primarily due to their length rather than extreme elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025807624/convento-jesus-del-monte-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convento Jesús del Monte Trail&lt;/a&gt;, at 9.4 miles (15.2 km) and taking over 4 hours, provides a longer excursion through varied terrain, suitable for those looking for a more extended walk.
tips: There are numerous legends about the Palace of Ambite and its thousand-year-old and gigantic holm oak: one of them is the one that tells us that a young and beautiful princess lived captive in the Palace, who, while waiting for a knight to save her from captivity, left tears fall on an acorn, which germinated on earth, giving rise to the great oak.
This source has an important historical significance, since the first inhabitants of the town settled around this spring, located in the heart of the alcarria.

This Vía Verde, which runs along the river of the same name in the Community of Madrid through a pleasant landscape of vegas, is very easy to follow, since all of it has an unmistakable red asphalt surface.

Take advantage of this bridge over the Vía Verde del Tajuña to rest in the shade, something rare on this route! These bridges were used to cross the old train track that is now converted into the green cycle path.

waypoints.name: Ambite Holm Oak and Palace of the Marquises of Legarda
